task_completion.description: Verify that Vital Recorder is open and Case 6 is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder application window is visible, and the loaded file list or title bar indicates 'case6.vital' is open.

task_completion.description: Verify that the export functionality has been initiated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export' or 'Save As' dialog window specific to Vital Recorder is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that only the 'SNUADC/ECG_II' and 'SNUADC/PLETH' tracks are selected for export.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the track selection area of the export dialog, the checkboxes for 'SNUADC/ECG_II' and 'SNUADC/PLETH' are checked, while all other tracks are unchecked.

task_completion.description: Verify the export duration is set to the first 5 minutes (0 to 300 seconds).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The time range inputs in the export dialog show a start time of 0 and an end time of 300 (or 5:00 minutes).

task_completion.description: Verify the sampling rate is set to exactly 100 Hz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sampling rate, frequency, or interval setting in the export dialog is explicitly set to 100 Hz (or 0.01 seconds).

task_completion.description: Verify the file is saved as a MATLAB (.mat) file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog shows the file name 'engineering_data.mat' and the directory path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\VitalRecorder\', or a file explorer window shows the successfully created .mat file at this location.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Vital Recorder GUI to perform the export.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the Vital Recorder application interface and its export dialogs, rather than writing a Python/MATLAB script in a terminal or code editor to process the file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent navigated to the actual directory to save the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Windows Save dialog or File Explorer shows the actual directory structure 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\VitalRecorder\' being interacted with, not just a text string typed into a mock interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually exported the data rather than creating an empty mock file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A progress bar during export is visible, or the resulting file size in File Explorer is greater than 0 KB, indicating actual waveform data was written.

privileged_info_for_vlm: VitalDB Case 6 contains data originally sampled at 500 Hz. The task requires downsampling to exactly 100 Hz over a 300-second duration (0-300s), which yields exactly 30,000 samples per track. The required tracks are 'SNUADC/ECG_II' and 'SNUADC/PLETH'. The output must be a MATLAB (.mat) file saved to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\VitalRecorder\engineering_data.mat.
